_comment: Manual patches applied on top of the generated command_schema.json.
Use 'python scripts/generate_command_schema.py ... --patches scripts/schema_patches.json'
to regenerate with these fixes preserved.
Each entry under 'commands' REPLACES the corresponding key in the generated command.
Only the listed keys are overwritten; all other generated data is preserved.
Patch categories:
1) Joint i/j marker groups: language.src incorrectly groups i_marker_name,
i_part_name, j_marker_name, j_part_name into a single 4-member exclusive group.
The correct Adams semantics are TWO independent groups: {i_marker_name, i_part_name}
and {j_marker_name, j_part_name}. Using both i_marker and j_marker is normal.
2) Force/function groups: language.src omits the {function, user_function} exclusive
group for force and data element commands. Without the group the linter fires E005
for the unused alternative.
3) user_function is almost always optional: it lets users replace built-in behaviour
with a custom subroutine. commands.exp marks it required, but in practice the
built-in function= (or force-function args) is the normal usage. Exceptions:
constraint create user_defined and simulation single_run subroutine consub,
where user_function IS the element.
4) j_floating_marker_name vs j_part_name: these are two alternative ways to specify
the J-body on direct forces. commands.exp marks both required with no group.
5) Spline file_name vs x/y: splines can be defined inline (x=..., y=...) or loaded
from a file (file_name=...). These are mutually exclusive. Result-set component
names are only needed when extracting data from result sets, not for inline data.
6) Spring/damper i/j marker vs part groups and coupler/motion_generator details.
See individual entries for rationale.
7) material create: language.src incorrectly marks youngs_modulus and poissons_ratio
as mutually exclusive. They are complementary elastic constants used together.
8) interface field set: database_fields and strings are mutually exclusive.
language.src omits this group.
9) xy_plots curve create: commands.exp marks all 8 dependent-axis args as required
with no exclusive groups, causing false-positive E005 for every alternative when
any one alternative is used. Fix: put all 8 in one exclusive group so providing
any one suppresses the others. Also: plot_name is derivable from the curve_name
path (e.g. .plot_1.curve_1), so it is not truly required.
10) view management orient: this command is absent from commands.exp entirely.
It is a valid Adams View command for setting the view orientation (up/forward axis).
Added as a new entry with: view (existing_object/View/required),
up_axis (enum, optional), forward_axis (enum, optional).
Also added to command_tree under view -> management.
11) data_element create matrix full: 'values' and 'result_set_component_names' are
mutually exclusive data sources (inline values vs result-set reference).
commands.exp marks both required with no exclusive groups.
12) i_marker_name / i_part_name / location and j_marker_name / j_part_name groups:
Providing an existing marker (i_marker_name or j_marker_name) already encodes
both the part and the location. commands.exp marks all alternatives required with
no exclusive groups on primitive joints, higher_pair_contact, and element_like
forces. Fix: {i_marker_name, i_part_name} and {j_marker_name, j_part_name} groups
on primitive joints and element_like forces; additionally {i_marker_name, location}
on higher_pair_contact point_curve (which has no j-body).

constraint create higher_pair_contact po._comment: i_marker_name encodes both part and location. {i_marker_name, i_part_name} and {i_marker_name, location} are two independent exclusive pairs. No j-body on this command.

data_element create matrix full._comment: 'values' and 'result_set_component_names' are mutually exclusive data sources. commands.exp marks both required with no exclusive groups.

material create._comment: youngs_modulus and poissons_ratio are NOT mutually exclusive — both are needed together to define elastic properties. language.src incorrectly groups them.

interface field set._comment: database_fields and strings are mutually exclusive — the field is populated either from a database query or from a literal string list, not both.

xy_plots curve create._comment: All 8 dependent-axis args (v*/y_*/d*) are alternative ways to provide the same data. commands.exp marks all as required with no exclusive groups. plot_name is also falsely required — it is derivable from the curve_name hierarchical path.

view management orient._comment: Absent from commands.exp entirely. Valid Adams View command for setting view orientation.

_comment_batch2: 13) contact create: commands.exp incorrectly puts stiffness/damping/dmax/exponent
in an exclusive group. These are all penalty-method parameters used together.
Similarly coulomb_friction/mu_static/mu_dynamic/etc. are friction params used
together. Both groups removed. penalty vs restitution_coefficient (group 6)
remains correct.
14) section create / section modify: cross-section dimension args (cyl_radius,
rect_height, rect_base, major_radius, minor_radius, ib_height, ib_base,
ib_flange, ib_web, xy_points, zy_points) are conditional on section_type.
commands.exp marks all as required, causing E005 for every unused shape.
Fix: set all to required=false.
15) geometry create shape force: joint_name, jprim_name, curve_curve_name,
point_curve_name are four alternative entity references (you use the one
matching the force element type). Added as exclusive group.
16) clearance create: i_flex is for flexible-body contacts; i_geometry + i_part
are for rigid-body contacts. They are mutually exclusive. Added exclusive
groups [i_flex, i_geometry], [i_flex, i_part] (and j equivalents).
17) file geometry write: type_of_geometry enum was missing catiav4, catiav5,
acis, vda — formats that Adams can export to.
18) part copy: part_name and new_part_name were incorrectly in an exclusive group.
Both must be provided simultaneously (source and destination names).
19) geometry create shape extrusion: profile_curve vs points_for_profile and
path_curve vs path_points vs length_along_z_axis etc. are alternative
definition methods. Added as exclusive groups.
20) geometry create shape revolution: profile_curve vs points_for_profile.
Added as exclusive group.

_comment_batch6: Batch-6: false-positive E005/E006 fixes applied directly to command_schema.json.
panel set twindow_function step — various args required=false, 4 exclusive groups
numeric_results create modal_polyline — exclusive group for state equation args
part create point_mass mass_properties — exclusive group
panel set acf_twindow reload — exclusive group {file_name, buffer}
panel set twindow_function impact — exclusive groups
panel set twindow_function simple_harmonic_function — exclusive groups
geometry create curve curve_trace — removed false exclusive group {trace_marker, base_marker}
force create direct modal_force — exclusive groups
interface data_table set row — range required=false; exclusive group {range, use_row_selected}
interface object_table set cell — exclusive groups
variable create — required=false for value args (real_value, integer_value, etc.)
_comment_batch7: Batch-7: stub commands added to suppress false-positive E001 for valid but undocumented commands.
vibration actuator create/input_channel create/output_channel create/vibration_analysis create/modify
